summary: utils-mf@12.0.1: keyword co-occurrence in minified bundle requires human review
details: Pattern matching on the package's minified index.js flagged the co-occurrence of child_process usage, an HTTP GET, and tokens resembling identifier reads on a single line. No traced execution path confirms exfiltration, command-and-control behavior, or install-time remote code execution. The package has no lifecycle scripts that auto-execute on install in the evidence available, and no specific attacker-controlled destination has been identified. Without a confirmed attacker-controlled endpoint, credential read path, or install/import-time trigger, the signal is too thin to publish a block, but the keyword combination in a minified bundle warrants manual review of the de-minified source before allowing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
